etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "noise")
etymology.interpretation: The character 喿(zào) represents the sound of multiple birds chirping in a tree, combining elements of sound (three mouths) and the setting (tree). It's the original form of 噪(zào, "noise"), signifying its use in contexts related to sound.
